purpose: M12.1 enables HIP-graph capture for chain_attn_mode='batched'. Diagnostic only: cycle-2+ wall is unchanged vs graph_mode='off' on this 1,840-kernel-per-pass DAG; the host overhead removed from the Python/ctypes path during replay is replaced by equivalent per-node overhead inside hipGraphLaunch on ROCm 7.x. Real win requires kernel fusion (M12.2+M12.3).

interpretation.graph_capture_cycle1_capture_overhead_ms: +17 to +25 ms (capture + instantiate + validation launch on second stream)
interpretation.why_graph_doesnt_win: ROCm 7.x hipGraphLaunch walks all 1,840 nodes during replay; per-node overhead is comparable to per-launch ctypes overhead in the direct path. The Python ctypes per-call cost is removed but immediately re-paid inside HIP graph's executor. Wins require reducing kernel count via fusion.

next_levers: Kernel fusion: w8a16_linear+argmax (streams top-1 token+value, eliminates rows*vocab logits materialization).
Selected-expert mul_mat_id consolidation: replace 138 MoE GEMV launches per pass with one ids-tensor GEMV per layer (~2 launches per layer).
Device-resident accept summary -> device-resident linear-state commit: replace 60 D2D hipMemcpy commits with one indexed copy kernel reading commit_rows[0] from device.
Build cycle metadata on device from previous cycle's accept output, eliminating 11 H2D copies per cycle.
